{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- family crest image -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Mihoko-no-Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}
|種別 = [[Shinbetsu]] ({{ill|Chigi (deity category)|ja|地祇|lt=Chigi}})
|出身地 = [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]
|根拠地 = [[Echigo Province]]
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Ōmi clan|ja|青海氏}} (Obito)
}}
{{nihongo|'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|久比岐国造|''kubiki no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kubiki kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled [[#Territory|Kubiki Province]].

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], '''Mihoko-no-Mikoto''', who shared the same ancestor as the {{ill|Yamato-no-Atai|ja|倭国造|lt=Yamato-no-Atai}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (''kubiki-uji''; [[kabane]] of [[Atai (kabane)|Atai]]). Its descendants include the {{ill|Ōmi clan|ja|青海氏|lt=Ōmi clan}} (''ōmi-uji''; kabane of {{ill|Obito (kabane)|ja|首 (姓)|lt=Obito}}), kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The Ōmi clan is recorded in the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' as descended from Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto.<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu, Ōmi-no-Obito", ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', [[Kōnin]] 6 (815).</ref>

== Base ==

=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then known as '''Kubiki Province''', centered on the western part of the later {{ill|Kubiki District|ja|頸城郡}} of [[Echigo Province]], corresponding to present-day [[Itoigawa, Niigata|Itoigawa City]].

Based on kofun distribution and place names like "Fukae", some locate the {{ill|Koshi-Fukae no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} in {{ill|Kanbara District|ja|蒲原郡|lt=Kanbara District}} or Fukae in {{ill|Nutari District|ja|沼垂郡|lt=Nutari District}}, and place the Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko's base in the area centered on [[Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu City]] and [[Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō City]].{{Citation needed|date=March 2022}}

Hōga Toshio argues that, since place names connected to the kuni no miyatsuko — such as Yamato River, Ōmi River, {{ill|Ōmi-machi|ja|青海町}}, and Ōmi Shrine — are abundant in Itoigawa City but not in Jōetsu or Myōkō, and since the place name "Fukae" is thought to have existed within Kubiki District, where the descendants of the Koshi-Fukae no Kuni no Miyatsuko also seem to have resided, the Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko's base should more properly be located in Itoigawa City.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter 2: Koshi and the Abe Clan", in ''Koshi to Izumo no Yoake'', Hōrei Shuppan, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>

== Tutelary Shrine ==
The {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|式内社|lt=shikinaisha}} '''{{ill|Ōmi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社 (糸魚川市)|lt=Ōmi Shrine}}''', presumably. It enshrines [[Shiinetsuhiko|Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto]], the kuni no miyatsuko's {{ill|ujigami|ja|祖神|lt=founding ancestor deity}}.

=== Related Shrines ===
* {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine (Itoigawa, Taira)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (''ōmiwa no kamuyashiro'')
*: A ''shikinaisha'' candidate enshrined in Ōaza Taira, [[Itoigawa, Niigata|Itoigawa City]]. It enshrines Mihoko-no-Mikoto (Mihoko-no-Mikoto), the ancestor of the shrine's Satō priestly family.
* {{ill|Ōmi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|lt=Ōmi Shrine}} (''ōmi jinja'')
*: A ''shikinaisha'' candidate enshrined in [[Kamo, Niigata|Kamo City]]. It enshrines Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto and [[Yamato-no-Ōkunitama|Ōkunitama-no-Kami]].


== Descendants ==
* {{ill|Ōmi-no-Fujin Magako|ja|青海夫人勾子}}
*: A [[Kofun period]] figure and court lady during the reign of [[Emperor Kinmei]]. The emperor sent her as an emissary to [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]], who had not been attending court.

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
* [[Echigo Province]]
* [[Itoigawa, Niigata|Itoigawa City]]
* {{ill|Koshi-Fukae no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:くびきのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:頸城氏|*]]
[[Category:越後国]]
[[Category:新潟県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:久比岐国造]]
